key_insights.insight: Couples often create patterns, figure eights, where one person's survival strategy triggers the other person's vulnerability.
key_insights.quote: The dance between the vulnerability and the survival strategy is one of the most common patterns in a relationship.

key_insights.insight: You go for people who express the part of you that you don't want to deal with; we outsource the feelings and needs we disavow within ourselves to our partners.
key_insights.quote: You go for people who express the part of you that you don't want to deal with...she is taking care of the feelings that you are disavowing or the needs that you are disavowing inside of you.

key_insights.insight: Instead of apologizing for absence, express thankfulness for your partner's presence to acknowledge interdependence.
key_insights.quote: If you actually said, I'm so thankful that you're here because I could not have stayed at the office late...And instead of apologizing, you thank. Then you are actually saying, I couldn't do this without you.

key_insights.insight: Modern loneliness stems from half-there-ness in relationships, where someone shares something important to a person who isn't fully present.
key_insights.quote: That's the beginning of a kind of modern loneliness actually, is that this idea that you can share something really important to someone who is half there, half there.

key_insights.insight: Ambiguous loss occurs in relationships when one person is physically present but emotionally absent due to distractions like phones and television.
key_insights.quote: When we live with this phone thing...there is less and less of a thread of conversation, of connection, of joy, of sex, of intimacy...Somebody is there, but they're not really present.

key_insights.insight: People are drawn to their partner when they see them in their element, passionate and competent, as a separate other person.
key_insights.quote: I'm most drawn to my partner when I see my partner in their element...It's when I see my partner as a separate other person that is already so familiar, but that is yet again, somewhat mysterious, somewhat unknown, somewhat elusive.

key_insights.insight: Instead of focusing on what couples are fighting *about*, its more important to consider what they are fighting *for*.
key_insights.quote: Instead of asking what are we fighting about, ask yourself what are we fighting for?

actionable_advice.advice: Interrupt patterns of disconnection by offering brief but meaningful acknowledgment to your partner.
actionable_advice.how_to: Stop working for 30 seconds, offer a kiss, hug, or gaze, and state you will be available in 20 minutes.

actionable_advice.advice: Replace apologies for absence with thanks to your partner for their support.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of saying sorry for being late, express gratitude to your partner for handling things in your absence.

actionable_advice.advice: Go for a walk with your partner and have a conversation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of telling your partner to stop something, invite your partner to go for a walk with you, side by side, to talk.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are feeling bored, create novelty and bring imagination to your relationship with your partner.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of saying I am bored, actively pursue new and unusual things with your partner.

memorable_stories: The anecdote about the podcast host and his girlfriend, about her habit of sending Instagram messages and his lack of acknowledging them.

The story about the speaker's friend who is going through a divorce; the friend said she and her husband "just fell out of love" after focusing on their business and raising a child, leaving their relationship as a residual beneficiary.

The speaker's story about how the insight her father gave her to 'look at the decency' when trying to get to know someone has stayed with her throughout her life. And that she traveled the world and experienced kindness by strangers.
contrarian_claims: It's not that opposites attract; rather, we choose partners who express the parts of ourselves that we don't want to deal with.
Science has decided that women need to be helped around desire, and men don't need to be researched because men don't have challenges around desire.
Sometimes affairs happen in happy couples, because people want to leave the person they have become and reconnect with their authentic selves
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Esther Perel (Love & Sex Expert):  Why Men Love Porn More Than Their Partner! It's Time To Enjoy Sex Again! The Real Reason Men & Women Cheat!
